title: BoundingBox2D Object
description: Transient object that represents a 2D bounding box. A 2D bounding box is a rectangle box that is parallel to the x and y axes. The box is defined by a minimum point (smallest x-y values) and maximum point (largest x-y values). This object is a wrapper for these points and serves as a way to pass bounding box information in and out of functions. It also provides some convenience function when working with the bounding box data. They are created statically using the create method of the BoundingBox2D class.

methods.name: classType
methods.description: Static function that all classes support that returns the type of the class as a string. The returned string matches the string returned by the objectType property. For example if you have a reference to an object and you want to check if it's a SketchLine you can use myObject.objectType == fusion.SketchLine.classType().

methods.name: combine
methods.description: Combines this bounding box with the input bounding box. If the input bounding box extends outside this bounding box then this bounding box will be extended to encompass both of the original bounding boxes.

methods.name: contains
methods.description: Determines if the specified point lies within the bounding box.

methods.name: copy
methods.description: Create a copy of this bounding box.

methods.name: create
methods.description: Creates a transient bounding box object.

methods.name: expand
methods.description: Expand this bounding box to contain the specified point.

methods.name: intersects
methods.description: Test if this bounding box intersects with the specified bounding box.

properties.name: isValid
properties.description: Indicates if this object is still valid, i.e. hasn't been deleted or some other action done to invalidate the reference.

properties.name: maxPoint
properties.description: Gets and sets the maximum point of the box.

properties.name: minPoint
properties.description: Gets and sets the minimum point of the box.

properties.name: objectType
properties.description: This property is supported by all objects in the API and returns a string that contains the full name (namespace::objecttype) describing the type of the object.   It's often useful to use this in combination with the classType method to see if an object is a certain type. For example: if obj.objectType == adsk.core.Point3D.classType():

classType.title: BoundingBox2D.classType Method
classType.description: Static function that all classes support that returns the type of the class as a string. The returned string matches the string returned by the objectType property. For example if you have a reference to an object and you want to check if it's a SketchLine you can use myObject.objectType == fusion.SketchLine.classType().
classType.return_description: Returns a string indicating the type of the object.

combine.title: BoundingBox2D.combine Method
combine.description: Combines this bounding box with the input bounding box. If the input bounding box extends outside this bounding box then this bounding box will be extended to encompass both of the original bounding boxes.
combine.return_description: Returns true if the combine was successful.

parameters.name: boundingBox
parameters.description: The other bounding box. It is not edited but is used to extend the boundaries of the bounding box the method is being called on.

contains.title: BoundingBox2D.contains Method
contains.description: Determines if the specified point lies within the bounding box.
contains.return_description: Returns true if the point lies within the bounding box.

parameters.name: point
parameters.description: The point to test containment with.

copy.title: BoundingBox2D.copy Method
copy.description: Create a copy of this bounding box.
copy.return_description: Returns the new bounding box copy.

create.title: BoundingBox2D.create Method
create.description: Creates a transient bounding box object.

parameters.name: minPoint
parameters.description: The minimum point of the box.

parameters.name: maxPoint
parameters.description: The maximum point of the box.

expand.title: BoundingBox2D.expand Method
expand.description: Expand this bounding box to contain the specified point.

parameters.name: point
parameters.description: The point to expand the box to.

intersects.title: BoundingBox2D.intersects Method
intersects.description: Test if this bounding box intersects with the specified bounding box.
intersects.return_description: Returns true if the bounding boxes intersect.

parameters.name: boundingBox
parameters.description: The bounding box to test intersection with.

isValid.title: BoundingBox2D.isValid Property
isValid.description: Indicates if this object is still valid, i.e. hasn't been deleted or some other action done to invalidate the reference.

syntax.python:
# Get the value of the property.
propertyValue = boundingBox2D_var.isValid

maxPoint.title: BoundingBox2D.maxPoint Property
maxPoint.description: Gets and sets the maximum point of the box.

syntax.python:
# Get the value of the property.
propertyValue = boundingBox2D_var.maxPoint

# Set the value of the property.
boundingBox2D_var.maxPoint = propertyValue

minPoint.title: BoundingBox2D.minPoint Property
minPoint.description: Gets and sets the minimum point of the box.

syntax.python:
# Get the value of the property.
propertyValue = boundingBox2D_var.minPoint

# Set the value of the property.
boundingBox2D_var.minPoint = propertyValue

It's often useful to use this in combination with the classType method to see if an object is a certain type. For example: if obj.objectType == adsk.core.Point3D.classType():
